initial_diagnostic_question.question: Does this ECG suggest the presence of lateral myocardial infarction?

criterion_selection.question: To accurately diagnose lateral myocardial infarction, which of the following diagnostic criteria should be evaluated?
options: Presence of diffuse ST-segment elevation in all leads
Presence of ST-segment depression in lateral leads
Presence of ST-segment elevation in lateral leads
Presence of an RSR' pattern in leads V1 and V2
Presence of ST-segment elevation in leads II, III, and aVF
criterion_selection.answer: Presence of ST-segment elevation in lateral leads

finding.question: Regarding the criterion you selected, does this ECG show ST-segment elevation in at least two of the lateral leads, including leads I, aVL, V5, and V6?

grounding.question: Which of the following leads show ST-segment elevation? Select all possible leads from the options below.
Within the selected leads, in which of the following segments can you observe the QRS complex with ST-segment elevation? The options below refer to time ranges on the ECG signal, provided in seconds.

decision.question: Based on the finding identified above, does this patient have lateral myocardial infarction?
options: Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis
